sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_dates (id INTEGER, event_date DATE, description VARCHAR(100));
INSERT INTO test_dates VALUES (1, DATE '1992-03-01', 'First event'), (2, DATE '1992-02-28', 'Second event');
SELECT id, date_trunc('month', event_date) AS truncated_date FROM test_dates WHERE event_date - DATE '1992-02-28' > INTERVAL '0 day';
SELECT id, event_date, (DATE '1992-03-27' - DATE '1992-03-22') AS days_difference FROM test_dates WHERE date_trunc('month', event_date) IS NOT NULL;

WITH date_summary AS (SELECT id, date_trunc('month', event_date) AS month_truncation FROM test_dates) SELECT month_truncation, COUNT(*) FROM date_summary GROUP BY month_truncation HAVING month_truncation IS NOT NULL;
SELECT t1.id, t1.event_date, (DATE '1992-03-27' - t1.event_date) AS date_diff FROM test_dates t1 JOIN test_dates t2 ON DATE '1992-03-01' - t2.event_date = INTERVAL '0 days' WHERE t1.id <> t2.id;
CREATE TABLE edge_cases (case_id INTEGER, boundary_date DATE, notes VARCHAR(100) DEFAULT NULL);
INSERT INTO edge_cases VALUES (1, NULL, 'Null date'), (2, DATE '0001-01-01', 'Earliest possible date'), (3, DATE '9999-12-31', 'Latest possible date');
SELECT case_id, boundary_date, date_trunc('day', boundary_date) AS truncated_boundary FROM edge_cases WHERE boundary_date IS NOT NULL;
SELECT case_id, boundary_date, (DATE '9999-12-31' - DATE '0001-01-01') AS extreme_difference FROM edge_cases WHERE boundary_date IS NOT NULL;
